critical.title: main.rs missing TangleProducer/TangleConsumer — on-chain job path is dead code
critical.detail: The runner never polls for JobSubmitted events, never submits results. run_inference in lib.rs will never fire.

critical.title: billing.rs verify_spend_auth_signature recovers signer but discards it (_recovered)
critical.detail: Any valid ECDSA signature passes. Auth bypass — anyone can forge SpendAuth for any credit account.

medium.title: BackgroundService::start() never sends Ok(()) — readiness signal broken
BSM implements inline interface copy instead of extending BlueprintServiceManagerBase from tnt-core
No service ID scoping in HTTP layer — no tenancy boundaries
run_inference hardcodes vLLM URL instead of using config
Private key stored as raw hex in config — no keystore integration
Temperature type mismatch: BSM uses uint32, sol! type uses uint64

verdict: Foreman's judges did not catch either critical issue. The auth bypass (discarded _recovered key) is a 1-line bug that any security-focused reviewer should find. The dead job path (no TangleProducer/Consumer) means the entire on-chain integration doesn't work. These are the gaps in Foreman's judge prompts.
foreman_gap: The judge prompts ask generic questions ('is billing secure?', 'are there panics?') instead of checking specific patterns. They need to: (1) grep for _recovered or unused variables in crypto paths, (2) verify the BlueprintRunner actually subscribes to on-chain events, (3) check that BackgroundService resolves its readiness future.
